_metadata.description: Framework module → TikZ diagram mapping for Phase 3 conversion. Generated by 7 parallel audit agents in Phase 2 of the Framework Lane Diagram Sprint.
_metadata.phase: 2 — review (awaiting user approval before Phase 3 execution)

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch01-generators-order
diagrams.caption: The five generators of Category τ in strict total order α < π < γ < η < ω (axiom K1). Each generator carries a distinct structural role in the kernel.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical visual for the five-generator kernel, directly illustrates axiom K1

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch06-spec-existence
diagrams.caption: The generative act: the single passage from the static kernel τ₀ (specification) to the populated universe τ (existence). The operator ρ unfolds all four orbit rays simultaneously in one indivisible act.
diagrams.rationale: Directly visualizes the core thesis: the single generative act that produces the universe from specification.

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch07-ontic-closure
diagrams.caption: The five pairwise-disjoint parts of the ontically sealed universe. The four countably infinite orbit rays and the beacon singleton {ω} exhaust Obj(τ) with no overlap and no remainder.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the result of the generative act: four orbit rays plus beacon, illustrating ontic closure.

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch10-rank-transfer
diagrams.caption: The rank transfer maps RT_π and RT_γ as depth-preserving bijections between orbit rays. Each map sends the element at depth n in the α-ray to the element at depth n in the target ray.
diagrams.rationale: Directly illustrates rank transfer bijections that earn the natural number structure on orbit rays and enable arithmetic operations.

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch20-coprojections
diagrams.caption: The fibration coprojections from τ³ to its base τ¹ and fiber T². The two readout projections π_base and π_fiber realize the 2+2 split of the ABCD coordinate chart; their domains become the macrocosm and microcosm in Books IV–V.
diagrams.rationale: Visualizes the structural decomposition of ABCD coordinates as a 2+2 fibration.

diagrams.label: fig:bookI-ch29-lemniscate
diagrams.caption: The algebraic lemniscate ℒ = S¹ ∨ S¹ as two polarized sectors joined at the crossing-point germ ω. B-polarized germs inhabit the left lobe; C-polarized germs inhabit the right lobe.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical visual for how omega-germs partition into B-polarized and C-polarized families.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch05-fiber-degen
diagrams.caption: Fiber degeneration of τ³ at the beacon ω. The generic torus fiber T² collapses to the lemniscate ℒ = S¹ ∨ S¹ as the base coordinate approaches ω.
diagrams.rationale: Directly visualizes the omega readout mechanism as the fiber limit structure at the profinite boundary.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch06-tau3-fibration
diagrams.caption: The fibered product τ³ = τ¹ ×_f T². The projection pr forgets the fiber coordinates (B,C) and retains only the multiplicative skeleton (D,A).
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates the coordinate decomposition underlying the construction of omega-germs from the stagewise refinement of the ABCD chart.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch09-hol-cont
diagrams.caption: Containment hierarchy of function classes on τ³. Analyticity (τ-holomorphy) implies split-complex holomorphicity, which implies continuity (II.T06), which implies measurability. Each inclusion is strict.
diagrams.rationale: Demonstrates split-complex holomorphicity as a distinct structural level forced by prime polarity.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch31-mutual-det
diagrams.caption: Mutual determination among the five central structures of categorical holomorphy. Each structure uniquely determines the others; the cycle has no free parameters.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical pentagonal visualization of the five-way equivalence theorem.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch43-yoneda-triad
diagrams.caption: The Yoneda triad: three equivalent perspectives on representable functors within Category τ. The natural isomorphism Nat(h_A, F) ≅ F(A) is the Yoneda lemma earned from the kernel axioms.
diagrams.rationale: Directly illustrates the Yoneda embedding; shows how Hom objects become τ-objects via point evaluation.

diagrams.label: fig:bookII-ch51-central-theorem
diagrams.caption: The Central Theorem isomorphism O(τ³) ≅ A_spec(ℒ) commutes with the bipolar decomposition. The top isomorphism is the Central Theorem II.T40.
diagrams.rationale: Visualizes the central isomorphism between holomorphic functions on τ³ and spectral algebra on the lemniscate boundary.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch04-enrichment-ladder
diagrams.caption: The canonical ladder of enrichment: exactly four layers E₀ ⊂ E₁ ⊂ E₂ ⊂ E₃ arise from non-emptiness, strictness, and saturation. No fifth layer exists.
diagrams.rationale: Directly visualizes the central thesis: the four-layer structure as the consequence of non-emptiness, strictness, and saturation.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch10-sector-decomp
diagrams.caption: The 4+1 sector decomposition induced by the boundary-to-interior functor: four primitive sectors A, B, C, D and one mixed (ω) sector.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical visual for how boundary functoriality produces the four primitive sectors plus the mixed Logos sector.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch19-trichotomy
diagrams.caption: The spectral trichotomy: every spectral character is classified as B-type, C-type, or mixed (X), providing the foundation for the Master Schema of Millennium Problems.
diagrams.rationale: The B/C/X spectral classification is the core machinery that frames all Millennium Problems as Mutual Determination instances.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch63-master-schema
diagrams.caption: The Master Schema: all eight Millennium Problems arranged across enrichment levels and sectors as instances of Mutual Determination. No free parameters, one architectural diagram.
diagrams.rationale: THE canonical diagram that frames all Millennium Problems as sector-level instances of the same enrichment functor.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch63-master-schema
diagrams.caption: The Master Schema arranged as the Hinge Theorem visual: each downstream book's results are sector-restricted instantiations of the same enrichment functor.
diagrams.rationale: The hinge theorem IS the master schema — shows every downstream result as a sector-level instantiation. Intentional reuse across spectral-algebra and hinge-theorem since both theses converge on this diagram.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch58-computational-bisquare
diagrams.caption: The computational bi-square at E₂: self-referential codes exist natively at the life-enrichment level where the τ-Tower framing gives P vs NP its structural interpretation.
diagrams.rationale: Directly illustrates why computation is native to E₂ and how the P vs NP bridge emerges from the bi-square structure.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIII-ch73-architecture
diagrams.caption: The architecture of reality: the enrichment-by-sector grid showing how the Bridge Axiom maps τ-structures to ZFC, with explicit scope discipline (established/τ-effective/conjectural) across each cell.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the Bridge Axiom's functor from τ to ZFC and the scope discipline grid across all sectors and enrichment levels.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch04-defect-bundle
diagrams.caption: Concept map for defect bundles: the topological data of T² determines a defect bundle D whose sections, charge classification, and holonomy group encode the quantum numbers of Standard Model fields.
diagrams.rationale: Shows how T² topology generates a stable defect bundle — the neutron emerges as the minimal such configuration.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch03-torus-arena
diagrams.caption: The torus arena T² as a fundamental domain: the square with opposite edges identified, with two independent cycles generating the homology H₁(T²;ℤ) ≅ ℤ².
diagrams.rationale: Establishes T² as the microcosm arena where the neutron stabilizes via bi-rotation locked by ι_τ.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch09-five-sectors
diagrams.caption: The five spectral sectors in concert: the universe of particles partitions into five sectors (Gravity, EM, Strong, α, Higgs). Every Standard Model field occupies exactly one sector.
diagrams.rationale: Beta decay reveals all five sectors in one process — this is the canonical visual for the Rosetta Stone thesis.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch67-coupling-ledger
diagrams.caption: The complete coupling ledger: the three gauge coupling constants g₁, g₂, g₃ are derived from ι_τ, κ_D, κ_ω, yielding α, sin²θ_W, and α_s as ratios.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the complete derivation chain from master constants to the fine-structure constant α.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch70-mass-ratio-chain
diagrams.caption: The 10-link mass ratio chain from τ³ to m_e. The Epstein zeta function Z(4; iι_τ) extracts ι_τ⁻⁷ from the torus shape; the holonomy correction π³α² brings R₁ to sub-ppm agreement with CODATA.
diagrams.rationale: Central thesis diagram: directly depicts the Epstein zeta at s=4, lemniscate capacity, and the complete derivation of electron mass.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch25-ew-mixing
diagrams.caption: Electroweak mixing triangle: SU(2)_L and U(1)_Y gauge fields mix via the Weinberg angle θ_W to produce Z⁰ and γ. sin²θ_W is derived from ι_τ.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the coupling of weak and EM forces through the Weinberg angle with specific inter-sector coupling κ(A,B) = ι_τ³.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch34-sector-atlas
diagrams.caption: Sector atlas of Book IV: Standard Model particles classified by sector label. Each sector corresponds to one force of nature.
diagrams.rationale: Comprehensive visualization of how 5 forces are architecturally organized in the sector structure.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch44-sm-tau-dict
diagrams.caption: SM–τ³ translation dictionary: each Standard Model concept corresponds to a precise structural counterpart in the categorical framework.
diagrams.rationale: Maps each nuclear particle to its τ³ correspondent, showing how nuclear physics derives from strong-sector confinement rules.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch35-three-gen
diagrams.caption: Three generations from the topology of T²: the first homology group H₁(τ³;ℤ) ≅ ℤ³ has rank three, and the three primitive winding classes correspond bijectively to the three generations.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical visual: demonstrates π₁(τ³) rank 3 directly, showing the topological necessity of three generations.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch55-complexity
diagrams.caption: Complexity hierarchy in the τ³ framework: the spectral structure identifies the P vs NP boundary with the diagonal-free discipline of the coherence kernel.
diagrams.rationale: Visualizes the complexity hierarchy from atomic (P) through molecular (NP), showing how chemistry bootstraps from electron structure.

diagrams.label: fig:bookIV-ch73-self-desc
diagrams.caption: Self-describing closure: Category τ is its own model — the framework that describes physical structure is itself an object within that structure.
diagrams.rationale: Direct visual of the capstone thesis: τ³ describes its own structure as a closure loop.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch01-fiber-base-split
diagrams.caption: The fiber–base split of τ³: Book IV studies the torus fiber T² (quantum/particle physics), while Book V studies the macrocosm base τ¹ (gravity, cosmology, large-scale universe).
diagrams.rationale: Directly illustrates the Hermetic Principle thesis that fibered product partitions physics into microcosm and macrocosm.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch10-cosmic-stack
diagrams.caption: The cosmic stack API of Book V: the fibration structure of τ³ determines the τ-Einstein equations, which drive Friedmann evolution, yielding all cosmological observables from zero free parameters.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the pipeline from τ³ fibration through τ-Einstein equations to Friedmann evolution to observables (H₀, Ω_Λ, CMB peaks).

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch13-tau-einstein
diagrams.caption: The τ-Einstein equation as boundary identity: the lemniscate boundary condition, together with the kernel axioms, earns the metric g^(τ)_μν and forces the Einstein field equations G_μν = 8πG T_μν as a categorical consequence.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly illustrates 'gravity earned' — boundary ℒ + axioms → metric → field equations in the boundary holonomy algebra.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch11-tau-orthodox
diagrams.caption: The τ-native to orthodox shadow correspondence: each fundamental structure in the categorical framework has a classical orthodox counterpart, but the τ-native formulation derives these from first principles.
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates convergence of two independent routes on the same gravitational physics through different paths.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch21-entropy-split
diagrams.caption: Entropy splitting in the macrocosm: total entropy splits into defect entropy S_def (which decreases as structure forms, opposite to the classical second law) and refinement entropy S_ref (which increases without bound). The two are dual under the base–fiber symmetry of τ³.
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates thermodynamic inversion: heat-as-electromagnetic through entropy decomposition into defect + refinement components.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch37-capacity-rotation
diagrams.caption: Derivation chain from capacity gradient to flat rotation curves: the capacity equation for τ¹ yields a gradient force that, combined with baryonic mass M_b, predicts asymptotically flat galaxy rotation curves with no dark matter parameter.
diagrams.rationale: Directly illustrates the no-dark-matter thesis: capacity equation → gradient force → flat rotation curves from τ-Einstein geometry.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch44-sector-budget
diagrams.caption: Sector exhaustion budget: the five spectral sectors of Category τ partition the total coupling budget of τ³. No sixth sector exists by the No-Fifth-Sector Theorem.
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates no-dark-matter from sector exhaustion: all five sectors accounted for, no sixth sector remains for dark matter.

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch50-bh-topology
diagrams.caption: Black hole topological event in Book V. In the τ³ framework the horizon topology is T² (non-contractible, no singularity); the orthodox S² horizon collapses to a singularity because the sphere is simply connected.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly illustrates black holes as topological events with torus horizons (T² not S²).

diagrams.label: fig:bookV-ch48-threshold-ladder
diagrams.caption: The threshold ladder of Book V: energy scales from the Planck scale to the cosmological horizon form a strict ordering determined by the enrichment structure of τ³.
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates the threshold structure for falsification: the energy-scale ladder provides the framework for specific tests across CMB, rotation curves, BH mergers, BBN.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch03-parity-bridge
diagrams.caption: The parity bridge as A→B→C morphism chain showing the physics-to-life transition via polarity seed.
diagrams.rationale: Shows the physics-to-life transition at the heart of the self-decoding distinction definition.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch16-homochirality
diagrams.caption: Four-force cascade amplifying parity into biochemical preference: the physical structure that enables life's asymmetric biochemistry.
diagrams.rationale: Complements the self-decoding distinction definition by showing physics-level enablement of life's homochirality.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch48-distinction-matrix
diagrams.caption: The Distinction/SelfDesc 2×2 matrix: Quadrant III (Distinction only = bounded-but-not-alive) vs Quadrant IV (Distinction + SelfDesc = alive). Visual proof of layer separation.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly visualizes that SelfDesc is strictly stronger than Distinction.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch50-enrichment-regimes
diagrams.caption: Enrichment regimes across the E₀–E₃ ladder, showing SelfDesc adds exactly one irreducible predicate at the E₁→E₂ transition.
diagrams.rationale: Contextualizes layer separation within the full enrichment ladder.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch09-seven-hallmarks
diagrams.caption: The bijection φ mapping each classical hallmark of life to a categorical predicate: Metabolism↔energy morphism, Self-replication↔endomorphism, and so on.
diagrams.rationale: THE canonical diagram for the inversion thesis: hallmarks follow as theorems from Distinction + SelfDesc.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch07-sector-template
diagrams.caption: The 4+1 sector template at E₂: five boxed sectors (A, B, C, D, ω). Instantiates Book III's abstract template at the Life layer.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical taxonomy diagram for the 4+1 life sectors.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch27-central-dogma
diagrams.caption: The Central Dogma as morphism composition: DNA → RNA → Protein with replication as self-loop. The genetic code is a BSD-type structure.
diagrams.rationale: THE canonical diagram reinterpreting the Central Dogma as a 2-step categorical functor.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch51-consciousness-boundary
diagrams.caption: The extended lemniscate ℒ_ext = ℒ ∪ γ_self with a self-referential loop at the boundary crossing. Neural systems implement structural self-models via Eval²(Eval).
diagrams.rationale: Illustrates how neural architecture instantiates the consumer sector and bridges to consciousness via self-reference.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch48-distinction-matrix
diagrams.caption: The Distinction/SelfDesc matrix showing how BH carriers occupy Quadrant IV (alive). Classificatory framework for cosmic life.
diagrams.rationale: Shows black holes satisfy the life definition at the structural level.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVI-ch50-enrichment-regimes
diagrams.caption: Enrichment regimes showing BH-life as a distinct identity regime (phenotype-stable, genotype-stable, immortal).
diagrams.rationale: Contextualizes the Crossing-Limit Theorem result within the enrichment ladder.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch05-sector-e3
diagrams.caption: The 4+1 sector template at enrichment level E₃: four registers (ontology/phenomenology, pattern/language/proof, dignity/ethics/society, mind/consciousness/Logos) plus the mixed Logos sector.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly illustrates the four registers plus mixed Logos sector at the core of E₃ decomposition.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch04-enrichment-tower
diagrams.caption: The enrichment tower culminating at E₃: mathematical kernel (E₀), physics (E₁), and life (E₂) provide the substrate for the metaphysical enrichment layer (E₃).
diagrams.rationale: Provides context and motivation for why E₃ is the terminal layer and why four registers emerge.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch17-generators-lemniscate
diagrams.caption: The five generators of Category τ on the lemniscate ℒ = S¹ ∨ S¹: α (complementarity) and π (projection) govern the left lobe; η and ω govern the right lobe; γ (crossing) is localized at the shared wedge point.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly shows the τ-kernel ontological foundation and relational primacy via the lemniscate structure.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch33-knowledge-sections
diagrams.caption: Knowledge as sections of a sheaf: local knowledge claims over domains U₁, U₂, U₃ are compatible on overlaps and glue to a unique global section. Epistemological coherence is the sheaf condition.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly illustrates perception as section-filtering and the sheaf-theoretic phenomenology framework.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch77-ci-sheaf
diagrams.caption: The Categorical Imperative as a sheaf condition: local moral obligations at the level of individual acts, communities, and institutions are compatible on overlaps and assemble into a unique global moral law. Kant's universalizability is the sheaf gluing axiom.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly implements Kant's CI as sheaf structure, showing universalization as gluing.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch77-ci-failures
diagrams.caption: CI failure modes: the Categorical Imperative can fail by producing a non-universalizable maxim (global section does not exist) or by self-defeating performative contradiction. Both are sheaf-theoretic obstructions.
diagrams.rationale: Complementary: shows obstruction theory dual to the positive sheaf condition, illustrating dignity modality failures.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch94-social-ontology
diagrams.caption: Social ontology as recognition-topology: a social group is constituted by a network of mutual recognition relations among agents. The category-theoretic structure is a directed graph whose cycles encode collective identity.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly shows how societies are organized through recognition topology.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch106-minds-topoi
diagrams.caption: Minds as internal topoi: the mind is modeled as an internal topos of τ³, whose external aspect is objective physical structure and whose internal aspect is subjective conscious experience. The two are categorically dual.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly implements consciousness as sheaf-theoretic gluing of local representations via an internal topos.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch112-free-will
diagrams.caption: Free will as branching in the action category: from a decision point, multiple action morphisms are possible, but only one is actualized. Non-actualized morphisms remain as genuine possibilities in the categorical structure.
diagrams.rationale: Supporting: illustrates how consciousness supports genuine choice via branching in self-modeling order.

diagrams.label: fig:bookVII-ch119-logos-sector
diagrams.caption: The Logos sector S_L as the unifying principle: S_L = S_D ∩ S_C is the unique mixed sector intersecting all four domain sectors, providing the principle that binds them into a unified framework.
diagrams.rationale: Canonical: directly shows S_L as the unique intersection unifying all four sectors where proof meets commitment.
